US: US1: Save and insert predefined responses in chats
AC: AC1: Add Saved Response button in Editor nav
Step:
1. Open a conversation
2. Verify Editor nav
Title: Saved Responses button placement
Expectation: - SHOULD display Saved Responses button next to GIF button in Editor nav

Step:
1. Open a conversation
2. Hover over the Saved Responses button
3. Verify tooltip
Title: Saved Responses button tooltip
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Saved Responses"

Step:
1. Open a conversation
2. Click the Saved Responses button OR press Ctrl/Cmd+K
3. Verify modal
Title: Saved Responses modal opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display Saved Responses modal

Title: modal X button exists
Expectation:
- SHOULD display X button in modal
- SHOULD close modal on X button click

Title: modal Search box exists
Expectation: - SHOULD display Search box (refer to AC3)

Title: Create new button placement and label
Expectation:
- SHOULD display Create new button in top-right corner
- SHOULD show label "Create New" on hover

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal with no saved responses
2. Verify empty state
Title: empty state message
Expectation: - SHOULD display empty state: "{image} Your saved responses list is empty. Start by creating your first one to save time during chats."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal with saved responses present
2. Verify list layout
Title: list left-side titles exist
Expectation: - SHOULD display list of saved response titles on the left side of the modal

Title: FREQUENTLY USED section hidden when no frequent responses
Expectation: - SHOULD hide FREQUENTLY USED section if user has no frequently used responses

Step:
1. Open modal with saved responses that have usage counts
2. Verify FREQUENTLY USED section
Title: FREQUENTLY USED section shows top 5
Expectation:
- SHOULD show top 5 most-used saved responses
- SHOULD sort by usage_count descending; when equal, sort by title ascending (A-Z)
- SHOULD show all available responses if fewer than 5 exist

Step:
1. Open modal with saved responses
2. Verify ALL section
Title: ALL section list and default selection
Expectation:
- SHOULD display all saved responses sorted by title A-Z
- SHOULD select first saved response by default

Title: arrow-key navigation in list
Expectation: - SHOULD allow Up/Down arrow keys to move between responses in the list

Step:
1. Open modal with saved responses
2. Observe right side panel
Title: right-side message detail display
Expectation: - SHOULD display title and message detail of currently selected saved response on the right side

Title: hover title shows message detail
Expectation: - SHOULD show message detail on the right when hovering over a saved response title

Step:
1. Open modal with a saved response that has a long message
2. Verify right-side panel scrolling
Title: long message scrollable
Expectation: - SHOULD allow scrolling in right-side panel when message is long

Step:
1. Open modal with more than 20 saved responses
2. Verify list pagination
Title: pagination loads 20 items initially
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 items per page
- SHOULD display Load more when user scrolls down and load next 20 items

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Verify footer
Title: footer shortcuts display
Expectation: - SHOULD show footer with shortcuts: X button or Esc closes the popup

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Press Esc
3. Verify modal state
Title: Esc closes modal
Expectation: - SHOULD close modal on Esc press

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Observe Search box
Title: search box default state
Expectation: - SHOULD display search box empty by default with placeholder "Search saved response"

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click/focus the Search box
3. Verify placeholder
Title: search box focused placeholder
Expectation: - SHOULD change placeholder to "Enter keyword…" when focused

Step:
1. Open modal
2. Type text into Search box
3. Verify x icon
Title: x icon appears when text entered
Expectation: - SHOULD display x icon when text is entered in search box

Step:
1. Type text in Search box
2. Click x icon or press Backspace
3. Verify search box and list
Title: clear search restores full list
Expectation:
- SHOULD remove search input and restore full saved responses list
- SHOULD hide x icon when search box is empty

Step:
1. Open modal
2. Type 1 character in Search box
3. Verify results
Title: search triggers from 1 character
Expectation: - SHOULD start searching from 1 character input

Step:
1. Type a keyword that matches title or message field (partial, case-insensitive)
2. Verify results
Title: case-insensitive partial search on title and message
Expectation:
- SHOULD return all saved responses where title or message contains the keyword
- SHOULD match case-insensitively and partially (keyword anywhere in field)

Step:
1. Type a keyword with no matching results
2. Verify results count label
Title: no results found label
Expectation: - SHOULD show "Search results (0)" below Search box

Step:
1. Type a keyword with matching results
2. Verify results count label
Title: matching results count label
Expectation: - SHOULD show "Search results ({number_of_matched_results})" below Search box

Title: search results list and default selection
Expectation:
- SHOULD display matching results list same as AC2 list format
- SHOULD select first result by default

Step:
1. Search with more than 20 matching results
2. Verify pagination
Title: search results pagination
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 results per page
- SHOULD display Load more on scroll and load next 20 items

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal with saved responses
2. Hover over a saved response message detail on the right side
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Verify submenu
Title: 3-dot submenu options
Expectation: - SHOULD display submenu with options: "Edit response" and "Delete response"

AC: AC5: Add a new saved response
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click + Create new button
3. Verify popup
Title: Create Saved Response popup opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display Create Saved Response popup with title "Create Saved Response"

Title: create popup X button closes popup
Expectation:
- SHOULD display X button
- SHOULD close popup on X button click

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup
2. Observe TITLE field
Title: TITLE field default state
Expectation:
- SHOULD display TITLE field empty by default with placeholder "Name this response for easy search"
- SHOULD be a required field

Step:
1. Hover over the info icon next to TITLE field label
2. Verify tooltip
Title: TITLE field info tooltip
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Used to organize responses. This won't appear in messages.."

Step:
1. Input text in TITLE field up to 140 characters
2. Try to input more
3. Verify character limit
Title: TITLE field 140-char limit
Expectation:
- SHOULD allow all characters up to 140
- SHOULD not allow input beyond 140 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 140 characters into TITLE field
2. Verify result
Title: TITLE paste truncates to 140 chars
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 140 characters when pasting text over the limit

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup
2. Observe MESSAGE field
Title: MESSAGE field default state
Expectation:
- SHOULD display MESSAGE field empty by default with placeholder "Write the full message you want to send to clients"
- SHOULD be a required field

Step:
1. Input text in MESSAGE field
2. Verify character count display
Title: MESSAGE field character count display
Expectation: - SHOULD display character count as "{X}/10000"

Step:
1. Input up to 10,000 characters in MESSAGE field
2. Try to input more
3. Verify limit
Title: MESSAGE field 10,000-char limit
Expectation: - SHOULD not allow input beyond 10,000 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 10,000 characters into MESSAGE field
2. Verify result
Title: MESSAGE paste truncates to 10,000 chars
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 10,000 characters when pasting text over the limit

Step:
1. Paste formatted text into MESSAGE field
2. Verify formatting
Title: MESSAGE field no text formatting
Expectation:
- SHOULD not allow text formatting
- SHOULD clear formatting when pasting a formatted text paragraph

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup with no input
2. Verify Save button state
Title: Save button disabled by default
Expectation: - SHOULD be disabled when TITLE or MESSAGE is empty

Step:
1. Input text in both TITLE and MESSAGE fields
2. Verify Save button state
Title: Save button enabled with both fields filled
Expectation: - SHOULD enable Save button when both TITLE and MESSAGE have input

Step:
1. Fill both fields
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save creates response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD add new saved response to the list
- SHOULD display toast: "New Saved Response created."

Step:
1. Open Create Saved Response popup
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify popup state
Title: Cancel button closes popup
Expectation:
- SHOULD always be enabled
- SHOULD close popup on Cancel click

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response to show 3-dot
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Click Edit response
5. Verify popup
Title: Edit Saved Response popup opens
Expectation: - SHOULD display Edit Saved Response popup with title "Edit Saved Response"

Title: TITLE and MESSAGE fields pre-populated
Expectation:
- SHOULD pre-populate TITLE field with existing shortcut
- SHOULD pre-populate MESSAGE field with existing full message

Step:
1. Open Edit popup with no changes made
2. Click X button
3. Verify popup state
Title: X button closes popup when no changes
Expectation: - SHOULD close Edit popup if no changes have been made

Step:
1. Open Edit popup
2. Make at least 1 change
3. Click X button
4. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: X button shows Discard Changes popup when changes made
Expectation:
- SHOULD display Discard Changes popup with title "Discard Changes?"
- SHOULD display text: "Are you sure you want to go? Changes have not been saved yet."

Step:
1. In Discard Changes popup
2. Click Cancel button or X icon
3. Verify
Title: Discard popup Cancel / X closes discard popup only
Expectation: - SHOULD close Discard Changes popup (return to Edit popup)

Step:
1. In Discard Changes popup
2. Click Discard Changes button
3. Verify
Title: Discard Changes button discards edits
Expectation: - SHOULD close Discard Changes popup and Edit popup without saving changes

Step:
1. Open Edit popup with no changes
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify
Title: Cancel button closes Edit popup when no changes
Expectation: - SHOULD close Edit popup if no changes have been made

Step:
1. Open Edit popup
2. Make at least 1 change
3. Click Cancel button
4. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: Cancel button shows Discard Changes popup when changes made
Expectation: - SHOULD display Discard Changes popup with same content as X button flow

Step:
1. Fill both TITLE and MESSAGE fields with valid input
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save updates response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD save changes to the response
- SHOULD display toast: "Saved Response updated successfully."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Click Delete response
5. Verify confirmation popup
Title: Delete confirmation popup shown
Expectation:
- SHOULD display confirmation popup with title "Delete Response?"
- SHOULD display text: "This response will be permanently deleted and can't be recovered. Are you sure you want to proceed?"

Step:
1. In Delete confirmation popup
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify
Title: Cancel closes delete popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the confirmation popup without deleting

Step:
1. In Delete confirmation popup
2. Click OK button
3. Verify result
Title: OK deletes response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD delete the saved response
- SHOULD display toast: "Saved Response deleted."

AC: AC8: Add saved response to chat box
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click title of a saved response OR press Enter
3. Verify chatbox
Title: response appended to chatbox
Expectation: - SHOULD append full message content into chatbox, placed after any existing text

US: US2: Create saved response from a conversation message
AC: AC1: Add Save this response button to Coach's non-text message
Step:
1. Open a conversation detail (workspace on Pro/Studio paid plan)
2. Hover over a non-text message sent by the Coach
3. Verify button
Title: Save this response button visible on hover
Expectation: - SHOULD display Save this response button between 3-dot and Reply buttons on Coach's non-text message

Title: Save this response button tooltip
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip "Saved this response" on hover

Step:
1. Hover over a non-text message on a workspace WITHOUT Saved Response feature (free plan)
2. Verify button
Title: button hidden on non-paid plan
Expectation: - SHOULD NOT display Save this response button when workspace is not on a paid plan with Saved Response feature

AC: AC2: Add new saved response from the conversation
Step:
1. Hover over a Coach's non-text message
2. Click Save this response button
3. Verify popup
Title: Add To Saved Responses popup opens
Expectation:
- SHOULD display popup with title "Add To Saved Responses"
- SHOULD display Title field blank
- SHOULD pre-populate Message field with the Coach's message

Step:
1. Coach's message is over 10,000 characters
2. Click Save this response button
3. Verify Message field content
Title: message truncated at 10,000 chars in pre-populate
Expectation: - SHOULD cut and fill only the first 10,000 characters of the Coach's message into the Message field

Step:
1. Fill Title field and verify Message is pre-populated
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save adds response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD add message as a new saved response
- SHOULD display toast: "Response has been saved"
